question: Define an abstract base class `Animal` with a pure virtual method `void makeSound()`. Implement two derived classes, `Dog` and `Cat`, each overriding `makeSound()` to print their respective sounds.

test_cases.expected:
Animals making sounds:
Woof!
Meow!
Woof!
Meow!

All animals have made their sounds and will be automatically cleaned up.

driver: int main() { std::vector<std::unique_ptr<Animal>> animals; animals.push_back(std::make_unique<Dog>()); animals.push_back(std::make_unique<Cat>()); animals.push_back(std::make_unique<Dog>()); animals.push_back(std::make_unique<Cat>()); std::cout << "Animals making sounds:" << std::endl; for (const auto& animal : animals) { animal->makeSound(); } std::cout << std::endl; std::cout << "All animals have made their sounds and will be automatically cleaned up." << std::endl; return 0; }
